title: 11 Returning Canadian and US Currency
body_text:
11	Returning Canadian and US Currency
11.1	Ordering Supplies for Returning Currency
Most of the products you need for returning notes and coins are supplied by Westkey Graphics (i.e., coin boxes, cash parcel bags, currency straps, and parcel tags). Deposit slips are provided by Davis + Henderson (D+H). You can order recap sheets and waybills from your armoured car company. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
Note:	Penny bags can be obtained from your armoured car company. Westkey Graphics and Royal Bank do not provide penny bags.
11.2	Returning Canadian Bank Notes
When returning Canadian bank notes to Royal Bank, prepare the notes in joint custody. Royal Bank prefers to receive one large bag of bank notes, rather than several small bags.
To prepare bank notes for return:
Group the bank notes separately according to denomination, in bundles of 100.
If you have less than 100 bank notes of any denomination, group all the loose notes, sorted by denomination, and secure them with an elastic band. Don芒聙聶t use elastic bands to secure each denomination separately and don芒聙聶t use a white currency strap for bundles with less than 100 bank notes.
Note:	Return $1, $2, and $1,000 bank notes to Royal Bank as unfit currency. All paper bank notes must be separated from polymer notes and returned to Royal Bank as Unfit Bank Notes. Unfit paper banknotes can be included with unfit polymer bank notes. See Section 11.7, Returning Unfit Canadian Bank Notes.
Secure each bundle of 100 bank notes with a white currency strap and record your financial institution芒聙聶s name, transit number, date of deposit, and teller number (if applicable) on each strap. The currency straps are self-sealing.
Group each denomination in multiples of ten bundles. Ten bundles are called a block. If you don芒聙聶t have ten bundles of a denomination, group the bundles in the number of bundles you do have. Secure the blocks or bundles with elastic bands at either end.
Prepare a deposit slip. The deposit slips are pre-encoded with your financial institution芒聙聶s branch number. Complete one for the total amount of notes shipped. Provide a breakdown of the number and dollar value of each denomination. Only complete the Cash (Incl. Coupons) table. The Visa and Cheques table is left blank. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Canadian Funds Deposit Slip
Place the original and one copy of the deposit slip flat on top of the block or bundles and secure it with an elastic band. If you don芒聙聶t have a copy of the deposit slip to forward to Royal Bank, send the original deposit slip and a photocopy.
Retain the extra copies of the deposit slip for your records. The deposit slip won芒聙聶t be returned to your financial institution as confirmation of receipt.
Place the deposit slip and block or bundles in the tamper-evident, self-sealing plastic bag.
Close the tamper-evident, self-sealing plastic bag. Attach a single-part tag to the outside of the bag.
Identify your financial institution芒聙聶s Royal Bank Cash Services Centre.
Record the total of the return; your financial institution芒聙聶s name, address, and transit number; and the address of your financial institution芒聙聶s Royal Bank Cash Services Centre on the outside of the cash parcel.
Have the people who prepared the cash parcel initial it.
Prepare the armoured car company芒聙聶s waybill and address it to your financial institution芒聙聶s Royal Bank Cash Services Centre (some armoured car companies use cash books rather than waybills. Fill out an entry in the cash book, if applicable).
Prepare and sign the armoured car company芒聙聶s recap form, record the date and time, and retain a copy of the recap form as evidence of receipt. Verify that the amount recorded on the cash parcel matches the amount on the recap form.
Transfer custody of the cash parcel to the guard.
Note:	Armoured car companies may refuse to transport a cash parcel that is improperly sealed or that displays evidence of potential tampering.
11.3	Returning US Bank Notes
Return US bank notes to Royal Bank according to the procedures in Section 11.2, Returning Canadian Bank Notes. US bank notes can be shipped in the same cash parcel as Canadian bank notes. However, note the following differences in procedure:
Group the bank notes, separately according to denomination, in bundles of 100. If you have bundles of any denomination with less than 100 bank notes, group the loose notes and secure them with an elastic band. Be sure to sort loose bank notes in their denomination prior to bundling and secure with the elastic band. Don芒聙聶t use a white currency strap for bundles with less than 100 bank notes.
Important
:	Unfit US bank notes are bank notes that have ink wear, tears, holes, folds, missing corners, stains, graffiti, and staples or tape. These bank notes must be accepted as regular fit US bank notes if the full bill is present, and its value is easily discernible.
Don芒聙聶t accept unfit US bank notes that have an unknown value (e.g., mutilated, damaged, contaminated). See Section 11.9, Returning Contaminated and/or Mutilated US Bank Notes, for more information.
Prepare a Deposit Slip / U.S. Funds. The deposit slip is pre-encoded with your financial institution芒聙聶s charter and branch number and/or location. Complete one for the total amount of notes shipped. Provide a breakdown of the number and dollar value of each denomination. Only complete the Cash (Incl. Coupons) table. The Visa and Cheques table is left blank. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Deposit Slip / U.S. Funds
11.4	Returning Canadian Coin
When returning Canadian coin to Royal Bank, prepare the shipment in joint custody.
Loose and rolled coin can be deposited with loose CAD notes and included on the same deposit slip. If the weight of the coin is substantial, create multiple deposit bags with individual deposit slips.
Note:	Pennies must be loose in a separate bag from other coins. Each bag should contain $25 of pennies for shipment. You can obtain penny bags from your armoured car company.
To prepare Canadian coin for return to Royal Bank:
Use commercial paper wrappers to roll coin in the following values:
Denomination
Roll Value
$0.05
$2.00
$0.10
$5.00
$0.25
$10.00
$1.00
$25.00
$2.00
$50.00
Fill the coin box according to its denomination. The Royal Canadian Mint has standardized requirements for Coin Boxes used to return Canadian coin. Canadian coin must be rolled and placed in the appropriate denomination box prior to shipment. The Coin Boxes have four spaces on the top of the box to affix your financial institution芒聙聶s identification stamp. The coin box form numbers are listed in the table below.
Order Number
Denomination
Quantity per Order
Rolls per box
Value per Box
S-06-05B
$0.05
50
50
$100
S-06-10B
$0.10
50
50
$250
S-06-25B
$0.25
50
50
$500
S-06-00-1B
$1.00
50
40
$1,000
S-06-02B
$2.00
50
20
$1,000
Ensure that the spaces on the boxes are used in sequence (i.e., 1, 2, 3, and 4).
Seal the box securely to ensure that any tampering is evident and affix your financial institution芒聙聶s identification stamp on the coin box.
Note:	Ensure your financial institution芒聙聶s identification stamp and transit number is clear and legible.
Prepare a deposit slip. The deposit slips are pre-encoded with your financial institution芒聙聶s branch number. Complete one for the total amount of coin shipped. Provide a breakdown of the number and dollar value of each denomination. Only complete the Cash (Incl. Coupons) table. The Visa and Cheques table is left blank. See Figure 3.
Important
:	Coin deposit slips must be prepared separately from bank note deposit slips.
Figure 3: Canadian Funds Deposit Slip
Insert the original and one copy of the deposit slip into an envelope or small cash parcel bag. Write 芒聙聹No Declared Value芒聙聺 on the outside of the envelope or bag.
Important
:	Don芒聙聶t put the deposit slip inside the Coin Box.
Retain the extra copies of the deposit slip for your records. The deposit slip won芒聙聶t be returned to your financial institution as confirmation of receipt.
Note:	Coin Boxes are not to be placed in a cash parcel bag or self-sealing plastic bag. Each coin box must be properly sealed and shipped separately.
Prepare two waybills for the armoured car company: one for the coin shipment and one for the envelope / small cash parcel bag containing the deposit slip. Ensure that the envelope is marked 芒聙聹No Declared Value芒聙聺. Address both waybills to your financial institution芒聙聶s Royal Bank Services Centre.
Note:	Some armoured car companies use cash books rather than waybills. Fill out entries in the cash book, if applicable.
Prepare and sign the armoured car company芒聙聶s recap form, record the date and time, and retain a copy of the recap form as evidence of receipt.
Transfer custody of the coin box and envelope to the guard.
Note:	Armoured car companies can refuse to transport a coin box that is improperly sealed. Coin Boxes must be sealed across the opening so that it can be determined if the seal has been broken or tampered.
11.5	Returning Canadian Fifty Cent and Silver Dollar Coins
These procedures apply to regular coins issued by the Royal Canadian Mint and not to collector or numismatic coins. Royal Bank does not accept collector or numismatic coins.
You can return small amounts of fifty cent and/or silver dollar coins to your Royal Bank Cash Services Centre. Send these coins as loose coin (i.e., insufficient amounts of coin to roll) and include them in the cash parcel bag for Canadian bank note returns. Also, list them on the Deposit Slip for Canadian bank note returns. See Section 11.2, Returning Canadian Bank Notes.
If your financial institution has a large amount or receives a large amount of fifty cent and/or silver dollar coins from a member, contact your Royal Bank Cash Services Centre before shipping. The Cash Services Centre will advise you on how to ship the coins (e.g., separated into different cash parcel bags or with specific values in each cash parcel bag).
11.6	Returning US Coin
Royal Bank won芒聙聶t accept returned US coin. If a small amount is inadvertently mixed in with Canadian coin, it will be processed at a Canadian dollar face value. Don芒聙聶t submit large quantities of US coin. US $1 coins are not acceptable for return. Don芒聙聶t mix them with Canadian coin.
11.7	Returning Unfit Canadian Bank Notes
Canadian unfit bank notes are returned to the Bank of Canada via Royal Bank.
Paper Bank Notes
All paper bank notes are unfit and must be returned to Royal Bank. Unfit paper banknotes can be included with unfit polymer bank notes.
Polymer Bank Notes
A polymer bank note is unfit if any of the following is true:
The windows or metallic images are scratched or damaged.
It is torn; has holes or staples in it; has stains, graffiti, or tape on it; or has folds or missing corners.
There is ink wear (i.e., areas printed in ink appear worn or cracked and/or print is increasingly faded, and text elements are difficult to read).
Due to the reappearance of counterfeit bank notes in older series notes, the Bank of Canada requests financial institution芒聙聶s cooperation in removing these bank notes from circulation.
The following bank notes should always be classified as Unfit Bank Notes:
Dominion Province of Canada bank notes
Bank of Canada $1, $2 and $1,000 bank notes
Bank note series 1986 or earlier, all denominations (this includes all Bird of Canada series notes)
Canadian Journey $5 and $10 bank notes without enhanced security features
Paper $50 and $100 bank notes
Paper $5, $10, and $20 bank notes without a metallic stripe
Polymer $10 bank notes of the Frontier series (issued in 2013)
Return Canadian unfit bank notes according to the procedures described in Section 11.2, Returning Canadian Bank Notes. However, note the following differences in procedures:
Don芒聙聶t use a white currency strap for bundles with less than 100 bank notes. Group the unfit bank notes separately according to denomination, in bundles of 100. Royal Bank prefers unfit bank notes in bundles of 100, when possible.
Secure each bundle of 100 bank notes with a white currency strap and record your financial institution芒聙聶s name, transit number, date of deposit, and teller number (if applicable) on each currency strap. The currency straps are self-sealing.
Note:	If you have bundles of any denomination with less than 100 Unfit Bank Notes, group the loose notes and secure them with an elastic band. Be sure to sort loose unfit bank notes in their denomination prior to bundling and securing with the elastic band. Don芒聙聶t use a white currency strap for bundles with less than 100 bank notes.
Write 芒聙聹unfit芒聙聺 on the currency straps.
Prepare a Deposit Slip for Canadian Unfit Bank Notes. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Canadian Funds Deposit Slip
Place the unfit bank notes and the deposit slip in a separate cash parcel or separate self-sealing plastic bag. Write 芒聙聹unfit芒聙聺 on the tag or the outside of the cash parcel.
Ship unfit bank notes via armoured car to your financial institution芒聙聶s Royal Bank Cash Services Centre via armoured car.
11.8	Returning Contaminated and/or Mutilated Canadian Bank Notes
Contaminated and/or mutilated Canadian bank notes are sent to the Bank of Canada on collection. Before returning contaminated and/or mutilated Canadian bank notes, read the Bank of Canada芒聙聶s Policy on Reimbursement of Contaminated and Mutilated Notes.
For procedures in handling and returning contaminated and/or mutilated Canadian currency, go to the Bank of Canada website (
www.bankofcanada.ca
)
Bank Notes
>
Contaminated and Mutilated Bank Notes
.
Note:	To return contaminated and/or mutilated currency, your financial institution must complete the Bank of Canada芒聙聶s Contaminated/(Damaged) Mutilated Canadian Bank Note(s) Claim Form.
Send the contaminated and/or mutilated currency as directed on the Bank of Canada website. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
11.9	Returning Contaminated and/or Mutilated US Bank Notes
The Bank of Canada does not accept contaminated and/or mutilated US bank notes. You can forward contaminated and/or mutilated US notes to your Royal Bank cash centre, who will ship them to Bank of America (BOA) on a collection basis only. The Bank of America will forward the parcel to the Federal Reserve after ensuring the required paperwork is completed. Your financial institution is responsible for completing the necessary instructions and documents, as Royal Bank simply forwards the package to BOA and does not physically open the parcels themselves. Payment for US dollar collection items can take well over a year.
Each shipment of contaminated and/or mutilated US bank notes must add up to a minimum of $100 USD.
The minimum fee for returning contaminated and/or mutilated US bank notes is $50 USD. The fee varies depending on the total amount of US submitted. If 1% of the total amount submitted is higher than $50 USD, BOA will use this amount as the fee instead. This fee will be charged on each collection form (shipment) sent to BOA. BOA will determine the cost of these fees, not the Royal Bank cash centre. The Royal Bank cash centre will complete BOA芒聙聶s collection form on your financial institution芒聙聶s behalf. Please ensure packaging guidelines are followed and any other required documentations are included.
Related Information
Visit the
Federal Reserve website
for packaging guidelines and required documentation.
11.10	Returning Contaminated and/or Mutilated Canadian Coin
To return contaminated and/or mutilated coins:
Complete the RBC 芒聙聯 Claim for Discrepancy in Cash/Coin Parcel Received, Form 2117 (write on the form that the coin is 芒聙聹contaminated芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹mutilated芒聙聺).
Important
:
Don芒聙聶t fill out a deposit slip. This transaction will be treated as a collection item and sent to the Royal Canadian Mint directly.
List the various coin denominations on loose paper (e.g., 30 x $2 = $60; 15 x $1 = $15).
Separate each denomination in its own zip lock bag and include an itemized note in each bag (e.g., $30 x $2 = $60).
Label the bag 芒聙聹ATTN: Currency Processing, Coin Desk芒聙聺.
Send the bag(s) along with the discrepancy form and the overall list of coin denominations to your Royal Bank cash centre. Royal Bank won芒聙聶t count the coins, but simply forward them to the Royal Canadian Mint.
The minimum aggregate value is $100. If any discrepancies are found, the Royal Canadian Mint will adjust accordingly. Reimbursement credit can take up to six months, but the Royal Canadian Mint will usually send an update within a few months.
11.11	Handling Counterfeit Bank Notes Received on Deposit
The Bank of Canada doesn芒聙聶t provide reimbursement for counterfeit bank notes. If you receive counterfeit bank notes on deposit, retain the notes and contact your local police department for direction. The instructions you receive may vary by region. Only contact the RCMP if you don芒聙聶t have local police in your region.
If a counterfeit bank note is received in a Royal Bank cash parcel, see Section 10.6, Handling Counterfeit Bank Notes in Incoming Cash Parcels.
11.12	Discrepancies in Returned Cash Parcels
Royal Bank does not perform a detailed count on bundles of strapped bank notes or coin received in incoming deposit cash parcels. Bundles of fit bank notes and rolls of coin are normally used to fill outgoing currency orders. Royal Bank ships bundles of unfit bank notes directly to the Bank of Canada. Discrepancies will, therefore, not be identified until the recipient verifies the content of the cash parcel or the Bank of Canada receives and verifies unfit shipments.
Royal Bank will only claim reimbursement for discrepancies found in deposit cash parcels that are greater than $20 Canadian and $20 US. If warranted, an adjustment will be issued once Royal Bank investigates the claim. Your financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account will be adjusted once Royal Bank settles with Central 1. If your financial institution disagrees with the adjustment, complete a trace request on ServiceNow as follows:
Note:	Contact Central 1, Client Support Services to request access to ServiceNow. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
Go to
https://clients.central1.com/
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 5.

Applications
. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre

ServiceNow
. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Accessing ServiceNow

Service Catalogue
. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Select Service Catalogue
Select the
Paper Payments
category, then select
Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request
. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: ServiceNow 芒聙聯 Paper Payments Category
Complete the Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request page. See Figure 10.
Note:	The 芒聙聹Requested by芒聙聺
field defaults to the name of the individual who is creating the trace request in ServiceNow. To change the name of the requestor, type the name of another ServiceNow user, then select the appropriate individual from the list of matches.

Copy and paste the link into another browser. Complete only Sections 1 and 2 of the form and save it to your workstation. Attach the completed form to the ServiceNow ticket.

Attach a scanned copy of the cash parcel芒聙聶s waybill and a scanned copy of the cash parcel芒聙聶s Royal Bank deposit slip to the Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request. Select the paper clip icon to attach the documents.

.
Figure 10: Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request
Once Central 1 completes the trace request, a response will be provided via ServiceNow, and your financial institution芒聙聶s account will be adjusted according to the request. If applicable, a service charge will be debited to your financial institution芒聙聶s account.
To expedite processing of discrepancies, you must ensure that your financial institution name, address, transit number, and date of deposit are clearly legible on each currency strap. Discrepancies will be identified and advised within 45 calendar days.
Related Information
RBC 芒聙聯 Cash Services Business Deposit Investigation Checklist,
Form 3790
11.13	Deposit Slip Adjustments
Occasionally, the total recorded on the deposit slip does not correspond to the total of the currency received in the deposit. When such errors occur, Royal Bank initiates a debit or credit adjustment. The deposit slip itself won芒聙聶t be adjusted.
END
